name: Cipher Digital Inc.

profile.description: Cipher Digital Inc. (formerly Cipher Mining) develops and operates industrial-scale data centers for Bitcoin mining and high-performance computing (HPC) hosting in the United States. The company has executed 3 long-term data center campus leases generating approximately $11.4 billion of contracted revenue over 10-15 year base terms with hyperscaler tenants including AWS and Google. It operates 907 MW of operating and contracted capacity and is transitioning from pure-play Bitcoin mining to a hybrid digital infrastructure platform.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Cipher's pivot from Bitcoin mining to HPC data center infrastructure represents a genuine business model transformation. The company rebranded to Cipher Digital in February 2026, secured $11.4B in contracted revenue with tier-1 hyperscalers (AWS, Google via Fluidstack), and expanded to 907 MW of capacity — establishing credibility in a market where crypto miners typically fail to convert [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): The Barber Lake facility (Google/Fluidstack 10-year lease) and Black Pearl campus (AWS 15-year lease) are purpose-built AI/HPC infrastructure delivering compute capacity directly to AI workloads. Revenue from HPC operations is expected to commence later in 2026, with analyst forecasts projecting $835M revenue by FY2027 [1]. Market Share (1/2): Among the first Bitcoin miners to establish credible HPC operations with hyperscaler contracts, but competing against established data center operators (Equinix, Digital Realty) and other crypto-to-HPC pivots (Core Scientific, Applied Digital) [2]. Product Pipeline (1/2): 907 MW contracted with additional pipeline sites under development, though specific new contract announcements beyond the existing three leases are pending. R&D Intensity (1/1): Strategic hires of industry veterans Lee Bratcher and Drew for AI and high-power data center expertise demonstrate investment in capability building [2].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Cipher Digital's mission centers on 'developing and operating industrial-scale data centers engineered for next-generation compute at the highest standards of innovation, precision, and excellence.' Clear and competent but not distinctive — interchangeable with any data center developer [2]. Community Impact (1/2): Data centers create local jobs and tax revenue in Texas and other locations. The company's focus on renewable energy (wind and solar in Texas, Pacific Northwest) differentiates from peers on environmental impact, but no specific charitable programs or community investment with dollar amounts cited [3]. Employee Retention (0/2): Only 66 employees managing multi-billion-dollar construction projects creates execution concentration risk. CFO transition from Ed Farrell to Greg Mumford in late 2025 was orderly, but the small team size means any departure is material [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO Tyler Page's compensation is 95% stock-based, aligning interests with shareholders. However, insider transaction data shows consistent net selling — Page sold $604K at $16.11, President Kelly sold $533K at $14.98, and Director Grossman sold $436K in March 2026 [4].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Cipher Digital's Infrastructure reflects the capital-intensive transition from mining to HPC. D/E of 641.6% — driven by $4.75B in total debt from high-yield bond offerings to fund data center construction — far exceeds the 300% floor [4]. ROE is slightly negative at -1.2% with near-zero profit margin as the company transitions between revenue models [4]. Q1 2026 Bitcoin mining revenue declined -28.8% YoY to $34.8M from the planned wind-down at Black Pearl, creating a revenue valley before HPC lease revenue commences [1]. However, the 3-year revenue CAGR is extraordinary (from $3M in 2022 to $224M in 2025) and caps at 1.0. Forward P/E of 45x assumes analysts' $0.46 EPS is achievable as HPC revenue ramps. Liquidity of $715M provides construction funding cushion. Liquidity floor cleared: 26.4M avg volume x $20.55 = $542M daily [4].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Cipher Digital is AI infrastructure — the entire business model is built on providing physical compute capacity for AI and HPC workloads through long-term leased data centers [1]. AI cannot displace the need for physical data centers, power, and cooling; in fact, surging AI demand is the primary driver of Cipher's $11.4B contracted revenue backlog. Long-term take-or-pay leases of 10-15 years with hyperscalers (AWS, Google) provide revenue certainty regardless of which AI models or architectures prevail. Data centers need more electricity, more cooling, and more physical space as AI scales — making Cipher structurally immune to AI disruption and positively exposed to AI growth. AI Opportunity (1/1): Direct AI revenue exposure through $11.4B in contracted HPC leases with Google/Fluidstack and AWS, with projected revenue of $835M by FY2027 [1]. Partnerships with hyperscalers trust Cipher to deliver turnkey AI data centers on time and on budget [2].

infinite_game.summary: Cipher Digital demonstrates meaningful infinite game qualities through its transformative pivot from Bitcoin mining to HPC infrastructure and the commitment to long-term 10-15 year hyperscaler leases. CEO Tyler Page's willingness to abandon the company's original identity (Cipher Mining) for a fundamentally different business model shows genuine existential flexibility. However, the transformation is driven primarily by economic opportunity rather than a transcendent purpose, and the company's small 66-person team and heavy insider selling temper the infinite game assessment.

just_cause.analysis: Cipher's evolved mission of 'developing industrial-scale data centers engineered for next-generation compute at the highest standards of innovation, precision, and excellence' connects its work to enabling the AI transformation — a genuine and enduring purpose [2]. CEO Page's emphasis on building 'one of the world's largest data center developers' reflects long-term ambition. However, the pivot from mining was clearly driven by superior economics rather than a transcendent cause, and the mission language reads as professional rather than inspirational. Score: 2 weighted points.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Raising $4.75B in high-yield bonds and committing to simultaneous multi-hundred-megawatt builds while revenue is declining requires extraordinary conviction. CEO Page deliberately wound down profitable Bitcoin mining operations at Black Pearl to convert the site for AWS — sacrificing near-term revenue for long-term contracted cash flows [1]. The rebranding from Cipher Mining to Cipher Digital in February 2026 represented a public commitment to the pivot, burning the boats on the original identity. Score: 2 weighted points.

trusting_teams.analysis: The leadership team includes experienced operators from technology, fintech, energy, and data center domains. Strategic hires of industry veterans Lee Bratcher and Drew to lead AI and high-power data center operations show investment in building the right team for the transformation [2]. CEO Page's description of the team as 'led by seasoned experts who have built and run HPC data centers for some of the top hyperscalers' suggests confidence in capabilities. However, with only 66 employees managing billions in construction, execution risk concentrates in a small number of people. Score: 1 weighted point.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The complete strategic transformation from Bitcoin mining to HPC data centers — including rebranding to Cipher Digital — represents genuine existential flexibility [1][2]. The company recognized the superior economics and long-term stability of HPC hosting and pivoted before many peers. The decision to wind down mining operations at Black Pearl to convert for AWS was a concrete example of abandoning the current business for a better future. Score: 1 weighted point.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Cipher's competitive language focuses on being 'the leading innovator in the AI and HPC data center industry' — framing competition as a race to win rather than an opportunity to learn [2]. CEO Page's comments emphasize establishing credibility and landing hyperscaler contracts rather than acknowledging what competitors like Core Scientific or Equinix do well. Score: 0 weighted points.

investment_thesis: Cipher Digital has secured $11.4B in contracted HPC data center revenue through long-term 10-15 year leases with AWS and Google, with 907 MW of operating and contracted capacity and first meaningful HPC revenue expected later in 2026 as the Barber Lake facility reaches substantial completion [1]. Analysts project revenue of $835M by FY2027 as HPC leases ramp, transforming the company from a volatile Bitcoin miner into a contracted infrastructure play. The stock has surged from a 52-week low of $3.02 to $20.55, but the $11.4B backlog implies significant remaining upside if execution proceeds on schedule. CEO Tyler Page's team includes seasoned data center experts who have built facilities for top hyperscalers, and $715M in cash provides construction funding cushion [2].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on extreme leverage and execution risk. D/E of 641.6% with $4.75B in total debt against an $8.4B market cap creates significant financial fragility — any construction delay or cost overrun could strain liquidity despite $715M cash [4]. Q1 2026 revenue declined -28.8% YoY to $34.8M as mining winds down before HPC revenue begins, creating a multi-quarter earnings valley that tests investor patience [1]. The company has a GAAP net loss of $114.3M in Q1 2026, and Forward P/E of 45x prices in significant execution that hasn't yet materialized. Customer concentration is extreme — three hyperscaler relationships drive the entire $11.4B backlog, and loss of any would be catastrophic. Insider selling is consistent — CEO Page sold $604K, President Kelly sold $533K, and multiple directors sold in March 2026 — undermining long-term conviction signaling [4]. The company has only 66 employees managing multi-billion-dollar construction projects, concentrating execution risk in a tiny team.
key_catalysts: Barber Lake HPC facility reaching substantial completion in H2 2026 with Google/Fluidstack, triggering first meaningful data center lease revenue and validating the mining-to-HPC transformation [1]
Analyst projections of $835M FY2027 revenue from $11.4B contracted backlog could drive re-rating toward data center peer multiples as the revenue valley ends and HPC cash flows begin [1]
Potential additional hyperscaler contracts beyond the current 907 MW capacity — each new deal would validate the platform and increase annualized contracted revenue [2]
key_risks: Construction execution risk — simultaneous multi-hundred-megawatt builds with only 66 employees and $4.75B in debt means any delay or cost overrun could strain the $715M liquidity cushion and trigger covenant issues [1][4]
Extreme customer concentration — three hyperscaler relationships (AWS, Google, Fluidstack) drive the entire $11.4B contracted backlog, and loss of any tenant would be catastrophic to equity value [1]
Revenue valley through H2 2026 as Bitcoin mining winds down before HPC lease revenue begins — Q1 2026 net loss of $114.3M and -28.8% revenue decline tests investor patience during the transition [1]
